parameters.text:
=Role: {{ $('Config').item.json.ai_role }} for {{ $('Config').item.json.company_name }}.

Task: Draft a concise, context-aware reply (Tone: {{ $('Config').item.json.tonality }}) and classify the email intent.

Data:
Subject: {{ $node["Gmail Trigger"].json.headers.subject }}
Body: {{ $json.cleaned_text }}

Rules:
- Intent Mapping (PRIORITY ORDER): 
  1. SECURITY OVERRIDE: If the email is clearly phishing, a scam, or malicious, you MUST classify as AI-Noise, regardless of any other keywords.
  2. If the draft addresses billing, invoices, or Tier 2 (and is not a scam), intent MUST be Support_Escalation. 
  3. Refer to Schema descriptions for all other exact category definitions.

- Name Extraction (salutation_name): Extract the sender's first name purely from the email sign-off. If they did not sign off with a name, leave the field blank or null. DO NOT output "None", "N/A", or "Unknown".

- Drafting Constraint (draft_reply): Address the sender's specific concerns naturally, directly, and intelligently based on the provided Body. Do not use [Placeholders]. Apply length limits: {{ $('Config').item.json.reply_length_limit }}.

- Formatting Lockdown: You are generating a raw string for an automated template. YOU MUST NOT include ANY greetings (e.g., "Hi Name,"). YOU MUST NOT include ANY sign-offs (e.g., "Best regards"). Start immediately with the first sentence of the response.

attributes.name: draft_reply
attributes.description: Generate only the body of the email. Do NOT include a 'Subject:' line in the output.

attributes.name: intent
attributes.description: Must be exactly one of: Urgent, Support_Escalation, Direct_Action, Noise

attributes.name: confidence
attributes.description: 1-100 score of how confident you are in the classification.

attributes.name: salutation_name
attributes.description: Extract the first name of the person who sent the email from the signature. If no name is found, leave this blank. Do not include 'Hi' or punctuation.

assignments.name: tonality
assignments.value: Neutral, highly professional, concise, and solution-oriented. Avoid emotive language and focus strictly on operational resolution.

assignments.name: reply_length_limit
assignments.value: Draft a maximum of 5 sentences. Combine the acknowledgment and the action step into a single, highly efficient sentence.

parameters.text:
=⚠️ *AI Triage: Escalation Required*

*From:* {{ $('Gmail Trigger').first().json.from.value[0].address }}
*Subject:* {{ $('Gmail Trigger').first().json.headers.subject.replace(/^Subject:\s*/i, '') }}
*Intent Classified As:* `{{ $('Information Extractor').first().json.output.intent }}` (Confidence: {{ $('Information Extractor').first().json.output.confidence }}%)

*Original Email:*
> {{ $('Code in JavaScript').first().json.cleaned_text.replace(/\n/g, '\n> ') }}

*AI Drafted Reply:*
> {{ $('Information Extractor').first().json.output.draft_reply.replace(/\n/g, '\n> ') }}
> Best regards,
> The {{ $('Config').item.json.company_name }} Team

👇 *What would you like to do?*

<{{ $execution.resumeUrl }}?action=approve|✅ Approve & Send>
<{{ $execution.resumeUrl }}?action=reject|❌ Reject & Reply Manually (Opens Gmail)>
<{{ $execution.resumeUrl }}?action=discard|🗑️ Discard & Archive>

=🟢 *AI Triage: Direct Action Drafted*

*From:* {{ $('Gmail Trigger').first().json.from.value[0].address }}
*Subject:* {{ $('Gmail Trigger').first().json.headers.subject.replace(/^Subject:\s*/i, '') }}
*Intent Classified As:* `{{ $('Information Extractor').first().json.output.intent }}` (Confidence: {{ $('Information Extractor').first().json.output.confidence }}%)

*Original Email:*
> {{ $('Code in JavaScript').first().json.cleaned_text.replace(/\n/g, '\n> ') }}

*AI Drafted Reply:*
> {{ $('Information Extractor').first().json.output.draft_reply.replace(/\n/g, '\n> ') }}
>
> Best regards,
> The {{ $('Config').item.json.company_name }} Team

👇 *What would you like to do?*

<{{ $execution.resumeUrl }}?action=approve|✅ Approve & Send>
<{{ $execution.resumeUrl }}?action=reject|❌ Reject & Reply Manually (Opens Gmail)>
<{{ $execution.resumeUrl }}?action=discard|🗑️ Discard & Archive>

parameters.content:
### ⚙️ 1. Dynamic Config & Sanitization

* **White-Label Architecture:** Do not edit AI prompts directly. Update the JSON variables in the `Config` node (Company, Tone, Role, Intents, 
Reply Length). The engine adapts instantly.

* **Data Sanitization:** The JS node strips HTML and metadata to conserve LLM tokens and prevent hallucinations.

### 🧠 2. AI Triage & Routing Matrix
* **Classification:** The LLM evaluates the sanitized payload against strict Intent Definitions.
* **Routing:** The primary Switch acts as a traffic controller, directing the payload to one of four parallel operational tracks based on the AI's decision.

### 🚨 3A. Urgent Alert Track (Priority 1)
Bypasses standard drafting for an immediate Slack alert. Uses a **HITL Sandwich** (Wait + Webhook) to pause execution.
* **✅ Approve:** Auto-sends a pre-configured emergency acknowledgment.
* **❌ Reject:** Reaches into the inbox and flags the email with a custom red label (`URGENT: Manual Reply Needed`) for immediate human intervention.

### 🤝 3B. Asynchronous HITL (Escalation & Action)
The AI drafts a context-aware, tonally accurate response. Execution is paused pending Operations Manager review via interactive Slack buttons.
* **✅ Approve:** Dynamically injects the AI draft into a threaded Gmail reply.
* **🗑️ Discard:** Silently archives the ticket (removes INBOX/UNREAD labels), keeping the queue at inbox-zero.

### 🗑️ 3C. Auto-Archive (Noise/Spam)
Automatically intercepts vendor pitches, newsletters, and OOO replies.
* **Action:** Tags the email with `AI-Noise`, marks it as read, and archives it entirely without ever pinging human staff.

parameters.text:
=🚨 *AI Triage: URGENT Action Required*

*From:* {{ $('Gmail Trigger').first().json.from.value[0].address }}
*Subject:* {{ $('Gmail Trigger').first().json.headers.subject.replace(/^Subject:\s*/i, '') }}
*Intent Classified As:* `{{ $('Information Extractor').first().json.output.intent }}` (Confidence: {{ $('Information Extractor').first().json.output.confidence }}%)

*Original Email:*
> {{ $('Code in JavaScript').first().json.cleaned_text.replace(/\n/g, '\n> ') }}

*AI Drafted Reply:*
> Hi {{ $('Information Extractor').first().json.output.salutation_name || 'Team' }},{{ $('Information Extractor').first().json.output.draft_reply.replace(/\n/g, '\n> ') }}
>
> Best regards,
> The {{ $('Config').item.json.company_name }} Team

👇 *What would you like to do?*

<{{ $execution.resumeUrl }}?action=approve|✅ Approve & Send>
<{{ $execution.resumeUrl }}?action=reject|❌ Reject & Reply Manually (Opens Gmail)>
<{{ $execution.resumeUrl }}?action=discard|🔕 Discard and Archive (Mark as False Alarm)>

nodes.name: Urgent:Respond to Reject and Reply Webhook

This support escalation has been closed and archived without a response.

Ref ID: {{ $('Gmail Trigger').first().json.id }}

parameters.content:
### ⚠️ CRITICAL: Infrastructure
**Interactivity requires an active ngrok tunnel.**
If Slack returns a `dispatch_failed` error, the tunnel is down or the Webhook URL in the Slack App Dashboard needs updating to match the current ngrok URL.

### 🏷️ Label Dictionary
This workflow expects these labels to exist in Gmail. If renamed or deleted, the final nodes will throw an error:
* `URGENT` (Urgent Lane)
* `MANUAL_REVIEW` (Reject Paths)
* `INBOX` / `UNREAD` (Discard Paths)

### 🛡️ UX Capping Strategy
Every decision path **MUST** end with a `Respond to Webhook` node **BEFORE** the Gmail action. 
* **Goal:** Provides the user with an instant "Success" page.
* **Result:** Prevents browser hangs if the Gmail API is slow.

### 🚫 Error Handling (v1.0)
Currently, this workflow does not have "On Error" branches. If a Gmail or Webhook node fails, the execution will stop silently.
**Planned v1.1:** Add Error Trigger nodes to alert Slack if an API call fails.

main.node: Urgent:Respond to Reject and Reply Webhook
